persons.arguments_against: Strongest case it is wrong: the deterministic place check is weak (all supporting events inherit their place), and 4 Skeen officials exist. Why it fails: the biography carries three distinctive initials 'G. J. A.' which match the stint name exactly - an extremely improbable coincidence for an unrelated namesake - and the position matches (sim 100) with the bio's asst-printer->government-printer arc reproduced in the stint (Assistant Government Printer 1877-79, Government Printer 1883 on). 'Government Printer' of Ceylon is a single office. The inherited-place weakness does not threaten a three-initial plus unique-post identification; 2 edition-years co-occur.
persons.would_overturn: Another G. J. A. Skeen in the printing line, or a date conflict in the asst-printer -> government-printer succession.

notes: Three identical uncommon initials plus exact position match and unique post override the weak inherited-place signal.
